_note: Per-area additions from Phase 2 post-training subagent (2026-05-04). PDFs for new papers were NOT downloaded by this subagent — Bash curl was sandbox-denied. Excerpts are populated from arXiv abstract pages (verbatim quotation) plus canonical equation forms cross-referenced from secondary sources (downstream papers, surveys); local_file is set to null for new papers and the excerpt files include explicit '[NOTE -- pdf-not-available]' markers where section/equation numbers should be re-verified. The deepseek-r1 excerpt was concurrently updated by the training-area subagent with verified PDF content; that file is intentionally left as written by them.

new_papers.title: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
new_papers.authors: Ouyang, Wu, Jiang, Almeida, Wainwright, Mishkin, Zhang, Agarwal, Slama, Ray, Schulman, Hilton, Kelton, Miller, Simens, Askell, Welinder, Christiano, Leike, Lowe
new_papers.summary: InstructGPT: canonical 3-stage RLHF pipeline (SFT -> reward model -> PPO with KL anchor). 1.3B aligned model preferred over 175B GPT-3 on user prompts. The reference for everything that 'RLHF' means in 2022-2024.

new_papers.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
new_papers.authors: Christiano, Leike, Brown, Martic, Legg, Amodei
new_papers.summary: Foundational preference-RL paper. Establishes that complex tasks (Atari, simulated locomotion) can be learned from <1% of pairwise preference comparisons rather than reward signals. Direct technical ancestor of Stiennon 2020 and InstructGPT.

new_papers.title: Learning to Summarize from Human Feedback
new_papers.authors: Stiennon, Ouyang, Wu, Ziegler, Lowe, Voss, Radford, Amodei, Christiano
new_papers.summary: Summarization with human-preference RL. Establishes the Bradley-Terry RM loss + per-token-KL PPO objective form that InstructGPT 2022 inherits and that all subsequent LM-RLHF papers derive from.

new_papers.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
new_papers.summary: PPO: clipped surrogate objective for policy gradient. The underlying RL algorithm used by InstructGPT, GRPO, DAPO, VAPO, REINFORCE++.

new_papers.title: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
new_papers.authors: Bai, Jones, Ndousse, Askell, Chen, DasSarma, Drain, Fort, Ganguli, Henighan, Joseph, Kadavath, Kernion, Conerly, El-Showk, Elhage, Hatfield-Dodds, Hernandez, Hume, Johnston, Kravec, Lovitt, Nanda, Olsson, Amodei, Brown, Clark, McCandlish, Olah, Mann, Kaplan
new_papers.summary: Anthropic's HH-RLHF paper. Training assistants jointly for helpfulness and harmlessness; introduces the HH preference dataset that became a standard reference.

new_papers.title: DeepSeekMath: Pushing the Limits of Mathematical Reasoning in Open Language Models
new_papers.summary: Origin of GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization): critic-free PPO variant using group-mean reward as value baseline. The algorithm that R1, Qwen3, Tulu 3 RLVR all use. Memory-efficient: removes the value model that PPO requires.

new_papers.title: A General Theoretical Paradigm to Understand Learning from Human Preferences (IPO)
new_papers.authors: Azar, Rowland, Piot, Guo, Calandriello, Valko, Munos
new_papers.summary: IPO: replaces DPO's log-sigmoid loss with squared loss on the preference gap. Addresses DPO's overconfidence pathology where the implicit reward gap can grow unboundedly while the loss vanishes.

new_papers.title: KTO: Model Alignment as Prospect Theoretic Optimization
new_papers.summary: KTO (Kahneman-Tversky Optimization): operates on single-rating data {(x,y,label)} with label in {good,bad}, not pairwise preferences. Asymmetric prospect-theoretic loss (loss aversion). Useful when only yes/no labels are available.

new_papers.title: ORPO: Monolithic Preference Optimization without Reference Model
new_papers.summary: ORPO: combines SFT and preference losses in a single objective (log-odds ratio of SFT loss between chosen/rejected). No reference model, no SFT stage - trains from base directly. Reduces compute and pipeline complexity.

new_papers.title: MAGPIE: Alignment Data Synthesis from Scratch by Prompting Aligned LLMs with Nothing
new_papers.authors: Xu, Jiang, Niu, Deng, Lin, Poovendran, Choi, Lin
new_papers.summary: Pre-query template trick: prompt aligned LLMs with only the instruction prefix (no question), have them fabricate the question first, then sample the answer. Near-zero-cost SFT data synthesis. 4M instruction set; beats UltraFeedback SFT+DPO.

new_papers.title: C3AI: Crafting and Evaluating Constitutions for Constitutional AI
new_papers.summary: Systematic study of constitution design choices: which principles matter, which transfer between models, which are model-specific. Begins automated constitution design.

new_papers.title: Reverse Constitutional AI: Controllable Toxic Data Generation
new_papers.summary: Reverse-CAI (April 2026): inverts the constitution for adversarial data synthesis. Probability-clamped RLAIF generates controlled toxic / harmful content for red-teaming.

new_papers.title: VAPO: Value Augmented Proximal Policy Optimization
new_papers.summary: VAPO: re-introduces a learned value function on top of GRPO-style RL, with shared backbone and value-loss-clipping. Reportedly outperforms DAPO on long-CoT tasks. Single-source result; cross-replication pending.

new_papers.title: VinePPO: Refining Credit Assignment in RL Training of LLMs
new_papers.summary: VinePPO: per-state Monte Carlo re-simulation as value estimate. 3x wall-clock speedup over PPO on MATH/GSM8K. Like applying GRPO's group-mean idea at every prefix instead of just the prompt.

new_papers.title: RewardBench 2: Advancing Reward Model Evaluation
new_papers.summary: RewardBench 2: 2025 benchmark suite for RM evaluation. Successor to original RewardBench. Open question (per Phase 1 sweep): does score correlate with downstream RLHF outcome?

new_papers.title: WARM: On the Benefits of Weight Averaged Reward Models
new_papers.authors: Ramé, Couairon, Shukor, Thom, Pendse, Adda, Vincent, Mary, Cord, Aubert, Cordier, Joulin, Jégou, Dehghani
new_papers.summary: WARM: weight-averaged reward models. Average parameters of K independently-trained RMs to reduce overoptimization and improve robustness. Adopted in Gemma 3 post-training.

new_papers.title: WARP: On the Benefits of Weight Averaged Rewarded Policies
new_papers.authors: Ramé, Ferret, Vieillard, Dadashi, Hussenot, Cedoz, Sessa, Pernias, Geist, Bachem
new_papers.summary: WARP: alternates RL training with weight averaging. Companion to WARM at the policy side. Used together in Gemma 3 post-training.

new_papers.title: Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overoptimization in Direct Alignment Algorithms
new_papers.summary: Overoptimization scaling curves for DPO-class methods. Shows DPO suffers from analogous proxy/gold-reward gap as PPO-RLHF. Contradicts early DPO narrative that 'no explicit reward model means no reward hacking.'
